opp.next_step: 6/2/26 MQ Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist. 4/28/26 MQ Per Julio, Antonino is traveling this week. Suggested reaching out 5/7 for update. 4/8/26 MQ RFP response submitted 3/31. Antonino and Julio are doing initial review of submitted proposals, specifically evaluating how each aligns with their medium-to-long term strategy. I am offering to provide business case to support their efforts. Antonino expects to have update on next steps the week of 4/20. 3/18/26 MQ Foundever has issued an RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management and Onboarding. Responses are due by 3/31/26. Foundever embarked in a Finance Transformation Project selecting SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud Edition for consolidating all ERP core Finance functionality across the Globe including the 42 Countries where Foundever has operations. The Implementation Project started January 2026 and will be executed in waves during next 2 years. First GoLive is January 2027 with 2 Countries and during 2027 and 2028 the remaining Countries to complete the 42 countries by the end of 2028. As part of the scope, not all the P2P functionalities were considered and Foundever is exploring with potential recognized names on the P2P spectrum for completing those and selecting, ideally one partner, for covering all the missing functionalities. The intention is having an initial estimated cost of licensing and implementation by the end of March for presenting a Business Case to Executive Committee. Once approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners for covering the missing functionalities, starting the implementation during 2026 for GoLive January 2027 of the first 2 Countries. 3/5/26 MQ Foundever is standardizing/migrating all 42 sites to HANA/S4. Initial sites are US and Phillipines and are slated to go live 1/27. They will be using SAP for transactional P2P and have proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down (Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY). Looking to Zycus for CLM, eSourcing and Supplier Management with potential for additional footprint after Coupa ramp down. Wants us to go-live 1/27 on initial sites with other sites implemented concurrently w/ ERP roll out schedule. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south. Julio is sending 2yr. overall rollout scope. 3/2/26 MQ Scheduled meeting on 3/5/26 with Julio for update of project status. 2/19/26 MQ Working with Mariana and Julio to lock down date for group for Supplier Onboarding demonstration. 12/29/25 MQ Followed up with Julio. He is OOO until 1/2/25. 12/3/25 MQ Held scheduled process timing/alignment meeting with Julio. He wants preliminary cost estimates for S2P suite vs. Supplier Management (onboarding) and eSourcing modules. Julio will gather metrics for pricing. He is checking schedules to see if it's feasible to schedule the follow up Supplier Management demo in December or wait until January.

items.quote: Our global CFO is... supervising us in his very few minutes that he has in his agenda.
We are also including some other competitor in the market that is... in equivalent level of you.
Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist.

metrics.narrative: Julio Lozano was explicit that cost is the driving decision factor: 'it's just pure cost... we are gonna go with the cheapest one for sure... but it's too soon for saying that this is gonna be the main criteria' (Avoma, 3 Dec 2025). No buyer-quantified savings or ROI figure has been confirmed; the only quantified figure on record (roughly $200k USD from consolidating 20M in local-currency spend) was a Zycus-side illustration during the 5 Nov 2025 demo, not a buyer-stated number. Marc offered to build a business case to support the Executive Committee presentation, which likely fed the RFP response submitted 31 Mar 2026.

champion.narrative: Julio Lozano is the clear day-to-day driver, confirmed by himself as one of the two people running the selection on behalf of procurement leadership ('Yes, it's the procurement leadership... I'm the one... engaging with the candidates and taking a look of the commercials,' 3 Dec 2025). He has been positive on the product: 'I really like the different functionalities and modules that you are showing today... very pleasant surprise' (13 Nov 2025), and Andreina Woodworth separately called a demo 'very thorough and very interesting' (13 Nov 2025). What is still missing: no evidence Julio is actively advocating for Zycus internally over rivals -- he explicitly told Zycus 'we are also including some other competitor in the market that is... in equivalent level of you' (13 Nov 2025) -- and there has been no direct contact with him confirmed since his 2 Jun 2026 status update. Developing, not yet a proven internal advocate.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent being displaced from P2P transactional workflows in favor of SAP S/4HANA, but is actively resisting Foundever's proposed 2-year ramp-down and pushing for full license-fee retention or a bundled-module deal instead of letting Foundever cherry-pick modules (Julio Lozano, 5 Mar 2026): 'Coupa is not happy with the rundown, and at the same time, really interested in offering only the modules that I mentioned.' If that negotiation fails, Foundever said it may need a temporary interim vendor rather than immediately expanding Zycus, which is the single most concrete near-term competitive risk. Separately, an unnamed set of RFP rivals was confirmed by Julio on 13 Nov 2025 ('some other competitor in the market... in equivalent level of you'); their identity and standing in the current down-select remain unknown.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-articulated and consistent across calls: today's e-sourcing tool (Coupa) requires an IT ticket to add evaluators and lacks bulk question creation (David Avila, 5 Nov 2025); supplier onboarding is described as a lengthy, manual process; contract management today is limited to a metadata repository, not full lifecycle management (David Avila, 13 Nov 2025); and legacy SAP ByDesign/Oracle approval flows required consolidation. Strategically, Foundever is consolidating its ERP to SAP S/4HANA and needs a partner to cover the P2P functionality gaps (CLM, sourcing, vendor management, onboarding) that S/4 does not cover, ahead of a Jan 2027 go-live for the first two countries (Julio Lozano, 5 Mar 2026).

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, or SOW discussion is on record. Julio Lozano raised a mutual NDA in the 13 Nov 2025 call ('I missed that part sending the mutual NDA to them') as a precursor to deeper discussions, but there is no confirmation it was executed, and the relationship has since progressed through a full RFP cycle without it resurfacing, suggesting it was resolved informally or superseded. Contracting mechanics remain entirely unscoped; this becomes visible only once a shortlist/award decision is made.

economic_buyer.narrative: Julio Lozano confirmed there is no global head of procurement since the prior incumbent departed the company around April 2025, and described the Foundever CFO's oversight as minimal: 'our global CFO is... supervising us in his very few minutes that he has in his agenda' (Avoma, 5 Mar 2026). Decisions currently run through a four-person procurement leadership group (two regional heads including Antonino Cappuccio, plus two category managers including Julio) with no single budget owner engaged by the relevant stakeholder_Step__c (18 Mar 2026) confirms the path still requires an Executive Committee business-case presentation before funding is approved. Move: get a direct or Julio-brokered introduction to the Foundever CFO or Executive Committee ahead of that presentation.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well-documented through the RFP stage: RFP issued 18 Mar 2026, response submitted by Zycus 31 Mar 2026, initial committee review by Antonino Cappuccio and Julio Lozano against Foundever's medium-to-long-term strategy (8 Apr 2026), an update expected 'week of 4/20' that slipped, a further slip to 'reach out 5/7,' and by 2 Jun 2026 still in 'reviewing submissions for down-selection to shortlist.' Beyond shortlist, the process runs to an Executive Committee business-case approval. The process itself has now run past Foundever's self-set end-of-Q2-2026 decision target with no revised date communicated.

decision_criteria.narrative: Confirmed criteria: cost-optimized, modular pricing (2-module vs. full-suite options explicitly requested, 3 Dec 2025); non-US (LATAM/APAC/Africa) multi-currency and multi-language sourcing capability (demoed 13 Nov 2025); bi-directional integration with SAP S/4HANA for vendor onboarding and contracting master data (5 Mar 2026). Formalized into a written RFP for CLM, Sourcing, Vendor Management and Onboarding issued 18 Mar 2026 with responses due 31 Mar 2026. The relative weighting of these criteria in the actual down-select has not been shared back with Zycus.

engagement.top_event: Re-validate the January 2027 go-live feasibility and confirm whether the close date 2026-08-21 supports it or whether the buyer has decoupled the RFP timeline from the S4/HANA project schedule. Ask Julio or Antonino: 'Your S4/HANA wave-1 go-live is Januar

CFO oversight is minimal. This creates decision drift and risk that no single person owns driving the deal to closure.

ESG reporting / spend analytics (iAnalyze) scope was flagged during a prior sales demo as needing must-have vs. nice-to-have clarification. If Foundever later decides iAnalyze is must-have and Zycus's iAnalyze capability does not meet their ESG reporting requirements, it could block the deal or force a scope/pricing revision.

Coupa is resisting Foundever's proposed 2-year P2P ramp-down and wants full license fees absent an agreement. Foundever called paying full Coupa fees a 'no-go'. If Coupa prevails, the budget available for the deal may shrink or Foundever may defer the decision until the Coupa contract expires.

Foundever's own internal target of a decision 'no later than end of Q2 2026' (30 Jun 2026) has now passed by 8 days with no confirmed shortlist. Against the buyer's stated Jan 2027 go-live, a compressed ~5-6 week window remains between shortlist, award, contracting and implementation kickoff if the 21 Aug close date is to hold.

hard.ais_why:
• The November 13 2025 demo (63 minutes) with Julio Lozano and six Foundever stakeholders is the strongest piece of evidence. Julio's reaction is captured as "Very, very pleasant surprise" at the feature set, and Andreina called the demo "very thorough and very interesting". The buyer engaged on AI-driven negotiation parameters, asking transparency questions on AI discount recommendations.
• A captured pain point reads "Need transparency on AI-driven discount recommendations to avoid overpaying". That is the buyer actively evaluating AI behaviour, not just being pitched. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake; AI importance is 'Important'; and the RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management was responded to on 3/31.
• Multiple Foundever attendees - Antonino, Julio, Mariana, Andreina, David, Sadoc and Ana - joined the AI demo and asked detailed AI-related questions on multi-parameter AI negotiation, smart onboarding agent preview, and autonomous negotiation with market benchmarks. Strong cross-functional buyer engagement on AI-specific capabilities underpins the Hungry call.
